meta.title: What's Your Rizz Score? | Charisma Quiz
meta.description: Measure your rizz through 12 social scenarios. Score 0-100 and discover your charisma archetype!

app.title: What's Your Rizz Score?
app.subtitle: 12 social scenarios to measure your charisma and charm
app.warning: This quiz is for entertainment purposes only and does not accurately measure real social skills.

q1.text: Someone attractive catches your eye across the room. What do you do?

options.a: Walk over confidently and start a conversation
options.b: Smile and make eye contact, wait for them to come to you
options.c: Look away quickly and hope they didn't notice
options.d: Pretend to be on your phone and avoid all contact

q2.text: Your crush sends you a vague text: 'Hey, are you free tonight?' How do you respond?

options.a: Overthink it for 30 minutes, screenshot it, send to group chat
options.b: Reply smoothly: 'Depends on what you have in mind'
options.c: Panic and leave them on read for two hours
options.d: Say 'Yeah, what's up?' — casual and cool

q3.text: You're at a party where you don't know anyone. What's your move?

options.a: Find the host and ask them to introduce you around
options.b: Stand by the snack table scrolling through your phone
options.c: Become the DJ or start a game — take charge of the vibe
options.d: Quietly observe and only talk when spoken to

q4.text: Your date accidentally spills their drink. How do you handle it?

options.b: Say 'It's okay' and quickly help clean up
options.c: Make a joke to lighten the mood
options.d: Laugh it off, spill a bit of yours too, say 'Now we're even'

q5.text: A friend asks you to be their wingman/wingwoman. What's your strategy?

options.a: Hype them up to the person, then smoothly exit the conversation
options.b: Try your best but accidentally make it about yourself
options.c: Just point at them from across the room and give a thumbs up
options.d: Decline — you're not great at that kind of thing

q6.text: There's an awkward silence on a first date. What do you do?

options.a: Ask a random deep question like 'What's your biggest fear?'
options.b: Smoothly bring up something you noticed about them earlier
options.c: Sit there and pray for the food to arrive
options.d: Make a self-deprecating joke about awkward silences

q7.text: A stranger compliments your outfit. How do you react?

options.a: Look down at yourself confused and say 'This old thing?'
options.c: Say 'Thanks! I like your style too' and start chatting
options.d: Smile big, say 'You just made my day', and keep walking

q8.text: Your ex's attractive friend starts showing interest. Your move?

options.a: Flirt back naturally — life's too short for drama
options.b: Be friendly but keep appropriate distance
options.c: Get flustered and change the subject
options.d: Ignore it completely and act like you didn't notice

q9.text: You need to make a toast at a friend's party. What happens?

options.a: You freeze up and just say 'Cheers, I guess'
options.b: You prepare something short and sweet, a little nervous
options.c: You tell a quick funny story that gets some laughs
options.d: You wing it and deliver a heartfelt speech that moves the room

q10.text: Someone you're interested in posts a fire selfie. How do you slide in?

options.a: React with a heart emoji and hope they notice
options.b: Don't interact — you don't want to seem thirsty
options.c: DM something witty that references the photo naturally
options.d: Just like the photo and go about your day

q11.text: Your friend dares you to go talk to someone cute. What do you do?

options.b: Walk over awkwardly and completely blank on what to say
options.c: No dare needed — you were already planning to
options.d: Accept the dare, go over, and somehow pull off a great conversation

q12.text: End of a great date. How do you say goodbye?

options.a: Give a warm hug, say something memorable, leave them wanting more
options.b: Awkward wave from across the car
options.c: Say 'I had fun, let's do this again' with a genuine smile
options.d: Just say 'Bye' and walk away quickly

legendary.name: Legendary Rizz
legendary.description: You have effortless, magnetic charm. People are drawn to you without you even trying. Your confidence is genuine, your humor is sharp, and your social awareness is off the charts. You read rooms like a book and make everyone around you feel special.
tips: Stay humble — your charm is a gift, use it to lift others up
Keep being authentic — people can spot fake confidence a mile away
Mentor your less confident friends — share the rizz wisdom

certified.name: Certified Rizzer
certified.description: You've got consistently strong game. You know how to read social cues, deliver the right line at the right time, and make people feel comfortable around you. You might not always be the loudest in the room, but you always leave an impression.
tips: Push your comfort zone — try starting conversations with complete strangers
Work on your storytelling — great stories amplify great charm
Practice active listening — it's the ultimate rizz multiplier

solid.name: Solid Game
solid.description: You've got a good foundation of charm that gets stronger the more comfortable you are. You might start slow, but once you open up, people see your genuine warmth and wit. Your rizz is a slow burn — and that's a superpower.
tips: Don't wait to warm up — try being your 'comfortable self' from the start
Practice confident body language: eye contact, open posture, genuine smiles
Remember: rizz is about making others feel good, not performing

undercover.name: Undercover Rizz
undercover.description: Your charm is there — it's just hiding beneath the surface. You have great qualities that people love once they get to know you, but you don't always let them shine in the first impression. Your rizz activates in small groups and one-on-one.
tips: Start small: give one compliment a day to build your confidence muscle
Prepare a few go-to conversation starters for social situations
Focus on genuine curiosity about others — ask follow-up questions

unspoken.name: Unspoken Rizz
unspoken.description: Your vibe speaks louder than words. You might not be the most outgoing person, but there's an intriguing aura about you. Some people are drawn to your quiet confidence and mysterious energy. Your rizz isn't about what you say — it's about who you are.
tips: Embrace your quiet strength — not everyone needs to be the life of the party
Work on opening up gradually — share one personal thing per conversation
Your mystery is attractive, but don't let it become a wall
